# TRAILER v2 — KEYFRAME PRODUCTION PLAN
**PLAN — IN REVIEW. No keyframes are generated by this document.**

> Scope: TASK-0006 deliverable (noted under TASK_QUEUE.md), CYCLE-0001 ③ Visual Production. Paper only — this plan sequences and gates the 16-keyframe batch that `studio/04_Storyboards/Trailer_v2_Storyboard.md`'s "Pre-production requirement" section calls for. It does not generate, approve, or lock anything. Every KF below ends in a per-KF approval gate per Handbook §15.8 and this cycle's TASK-0006 note ("YES (per-KF approval)" in TASK_QUEUE.md).
>
> Companion document: `studio/02_Art/Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md` (Deliverable 1, same cycle) — several KFs below are hard-blocked on kits spec'd there.
>
> Blocking context restated (do not generate against this plan yet):
> - **I-0001** — LOCKED masters are platform-hosted, thread-scoped, unchecksummed. Blocks unattended/durable generation pending TASK-0003.
> - **Missing kits** — Ha-eun, Si-woo, Cha (v3), and the Moderator have no production-ready kits (see `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md`); several KFs need one of these before they can be shot at all, not just approved.
> - **Q-13** (PMU ch.143 reference images never obtained) — relevant only as an optional completeness item, not a hard blocker; noted per-KF where it could matter.
> - **Q-14** (key-visual poster in locked v3 style) — a separate, not-yet-authorized asset; noted where a KF could double as poster material, but not assumed.

---

## Per-keyframe production table

Columns: **KF id** | **shot reference** (Trailer_v2_Storyboard.md shot(s) using it) | **required LOCKED assets** (named from Asset_Registry.md) | **missing/blocked dependencies** | **generation-packet checklist status (§15.7)** | **approval gate**.

| **KF-01** — crack sky (thin) | S02 (also referenced as the storyboard's own pre-production intro image before it's "grown" in KF-02) | None character-bearing — this is a sky/environment-only beat. No specific ENV plate is named in Environment_Bible for "open sky," so likely needs only the Art_Direction sky-crack visual law (diagonal 20–25°, jagged, branching, thin-at-first stage) as its reference, not a locked plate. | I-0001 (durability) only. No missing kit. Low complexity — thinnest crack stage per Art_Direction's progression law ("thin at first appearance"). | Environment ref: N/A (no plate). Character ref: N/A. Continuity restatement: sky-crack progression law (thin, diagonal, branching, never identical twice — this KF represents an EARLY progression stage, must be visibly thinner than KF-02/KF-03/any Ch.1-onward crack that appears in EP.1/EP.2 art, since it's a trailer-only condensed dramatization). Composition/camera: per storyboard S02, "ultra-slow push." VFX stage: FX-PATCH grammar (see Effects_Bible) but pre-detonation/thin stage only. Lettering area: reserve for "THE WORLD ENDS TONIGHT" card (HTML/Pillow overlay, not baked). Negative constraints: flat-2D law; no volumetric glow bloom beyond what Effects_Bible's FX-PATCH specifies ("blinding white core," but this is an EARLY/thin stage so restrained accordingly). | Per-KF creator approval (TASK-0006). Cheapest KF to unblock — no kit dependency. |

Han's kit is fully LOCKED (`CHAR-001-M/K1/K2/K3/V8`) — no kit gap. Silhouette treatment may reduce reference need to just outline/proportion accuracy from the master. | Same sky-crack continuity restatement as KF-01, PROGRESSED stage (visibly grown per Art_Direction's progression law — must differ from KF-01, not be a recolor). Character ref: Han master, restated as SILHOUETTE only (scar/eye-glow detail likely not needed if fully backlit, per storyboard's implied treatment — flag this reduction as a生成-packet decision for the artist, not assumed here). Composition: per storyboard, static/held wide against the grown crack. | Per-KF creator approval. Low complexity — Han's assets are fully unblocked. |

Also needs the match-cut companion (a 9.0 city-street "converting" treatment) which likewise has no locked plate — Environment_Bible lists "POST-PATCH damaged variants of every 9.0 plate (Ch.3+): torn sky light logic, debris, silence staging" as not-yet-generated. | Cannot complete a full generation packet today — environment reference is the blocking field. Rest of packet (FX-PATCH wave grammar: "environmental scale, no confetti... in: 1s flood · hold 3–4s · out: 1s recede") is already fully specified in Effects_Bible and ready to use once a plate exists. | Per-KF creator approval — but first needs a creator/④-REPO-flagged decision to commission the missing 8.0 settlement-wide + 9.0 post-patch street ENV plates as a prerequisite spec (out of this plan's scope to spec those plates; flagged to TASK_QUEUE/OPEN_QUESTIONS as a new gap, see "Ready order" below). |
| **KF-04** — split-seam Cha | S07 | `CHAR-004-M` (Cha dual master) is LOCKED. However, per `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md`'s KIT-CHAR-004 finding, her only EXPRESSION/pose kit reference (`f8b56b3a…`) is from the DEPRECATED v2 SL-flat era, which Asset_Registry.md's own blacklist appendix lists as superseded. | **BLOCKED on Cha's v3 kit** (per Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md, KIT-CHAR-004) if this shot needs anything beyond her locked dual master's default pose — a "split-seam" mid-rewrite pose (face/body caught between two eras, matching the prologue's "Cha is rewritten MID-SENTENCE" canon beat) is almost certainly a NEW pose not covered by the master alone. Also needs an ENV plate for the 8.0 settlement (same gap noted under KF-03) if the shot shows her environment context rather than an isolated character close-up. | Continuity restatement ready (wrench callus LEFT hand; identical face both eras; the specific mid-sentence-rewrite visual conceit is described in Master_Project_Bible §2 prologue summary: "Cha is rewritten MID-SENTENCE ('When the well's done, you should—' → '—come by before we close, student')" — this is strong existing canon to draw the pose from, just not yet spec'd as a POSE in any kit). Dialogue cards already scripted in storyboard (two-card split). VFX: could reasonably borrow FX-PATCH's "white rewrite wall converting terrain line-by-line" language applied to a character rather than terrain — this would be a NEW effect-application, not currently covered by Effects_Bible for a character subject, and should be flagged to the creator as an effects-grammar extension request rather than assumed. | Per-KF creator approval, gated behind Cha's v3 kit (Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md APPROVAL BLOCK) AND a decision on whether FX-PATCH's terrain-conversion grammar may be applied to a character mid-rewrite (new creative/technical question, not yet asked anywhere in existing docs — recommend adding to OPEN_QUESTIONS if creator wants to formalize it). |

Character-wise, this is likely a crowd/background-extras shot (no named-character kit required) unless a specific character (e.g. Si-woo, Ha-eun) needs to be legible in the freeze-frame. | Possibly blocked on a festival-night crowd/fireworks ENV treatment not yet locked (ENV-003 is bright-day festival PREP, not night fireworks) — needs clarification on whether ENV-003's existing plate can be relit to night/fireworks via a lighting variant (per Environment_Bible's "Per-plate variant checklist: day · sunset · night · rain · festival-lit · battle-damaged" — "festival-lit" IS an anticipated variant category, so this is a variant-generation gap, not a from-scratch plate gap, which is comparatively low-effort once I-0001 clears). If any named character must be legible in the freeze, add that character's kit as a dependency. | Continuity: needs to establish which (if any) named characters are visible. Effects: none specified for fireworks itself in Effects_Bible (fireworks are a mundane/diegetic effect, not a power-system FX-ID) — flat 2D treatment per Art_Direction's general style law suffices; no VFX-Bible entry needed. Composition: "freeze mid-burst," per storyboard. | Per-KF creator approval. Recommend creator/③ clarify which named characters (if any) populate this crowd shot before generation — currently under-specified even by the storyboard itself. |
| **KF-06** — Etude spell fizzling in a student's hands, panic (NEW) | S05 | Ambiguous which "student" — if Si-woo, needs his v3 kit (Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md KIT-CHAR-002); if an unnamed extra, no kit needed but loses characterization value. `FX-ETUDE` is LOCKED in Effects_Bible and already assigns gold specifically to Si-woo — using Si-woo here would let the shot double as characterization (his signature effect visibly breaking) rather than a generic extra. | **Recommend BLOCKING this KF on Si-woo's kit** even though it could technically be shot with an unnamed extra — using an unnamed student would waste the shot's dramatic potential (per Effects_Bible, FX-ETUDE's gold is Si-woo's specific signature; a random extra fizzling a gold-line spell would imply it's Si-woo anyway to anyone who's seen his kit elsewhere in the trailer, so consistency argues for deliberately casting him here). This is a PROPOSAL/creative recommendation, not an assertion of canon fact — flagged as such. | Effects: FX-ETUDE already fully specified — the "fizzling" state (Etude glitching per S05's "YOUR MANA IS NO LONGER SUPPORTED" plaque) is a NEW effect-state not explicitly in Effects_Bible's FX-ETUDE row (which describes normal operation, not deprecation-failure). This should be flagged as an effects-grammar extension: FX-ETUDE-FAIL or similar, describing what a "no longer supported" Etude looks like (recommend: the gold line stutters/pixelates using the same square-static visual language as FX-PATCH/FX-ROLLBACK for consistency, but this is a PROPOSAL). | Per-KF creator approval, gated behind (a) confirming Si-woo as the student (recommended) → his kit, and (b) a decision on the FX-ETUDE-FAIL effect-extension proposal. |

Student-extras running does not require named kits if unnamed. | **HARD BLOCKED — no monster design exists at any stage** (not even a DRAFT spec, unlike the Moderator which at least has a Character_Bible TODO entry). Silhouette-through-smoke treatment could reduce the immediate need to a rough silhouette only, but even a silhouette needs SOME agreed shape language to avoid a random guess. | Cannot build a generation packet — no design exists to restate as "continuity" and no Effects_Bible entry covers monster appearance (only combat-adjacent FX like FX-QI/FX-GOD which belong to characters' human power systems). | Cannot be scheduled for creator approval yet — needs a new TASK to spec at least a silhouette-level monster design first. Recommend adding to TASK_QUEUE/OPEN_QUESTIONS (see "Ready order" below). |

This is a legitimate sequencing option, flagged as a PROPOSAL for the creator to accept or reject. | Han's half: continuity restatement (scar LEFT brow, sword RIGHT hand — though this shot is one-handed CATCH, not a sword beat, so restate "empty catching hand" laterality carefully, likely his dominant/sword hand per the character's established economy-of-motion animation notes) is fully ready. Impact frame per Effects_Bible's "IMPACT FRAMES: 1–2 frame full-screen inversion... max 2 per shot" is ready to spec. Monster half: blocked, see KF-07. | Per-KF creator approval on Han's half is POSSIBLE ahead of the monster design (see PROPOSAL above); full-shot approval blocked until monster resolved. Directly matches an already-documented expression. | Continuity: "dry almost-smile (never reaches eyes)" per Character_Bible verbatim — the generation packet should explicitly restate the "never reaches eyes" qualifier since it's easy for a generic smile to drift into a warmer expression than intended. Dialogue card ready ("I'LL BE QUICK.") — matches his documented tic exactly. | Per-KF creator approval. Low risk. |
| **KF-15** — registry cards stamping REWRITTEN (NEW UI still) | S01 | No specific asset — this is a UI/graphic-design still (a "registry card" stamp effect), not a character or environment plate. Nothing in Asset_Registry.md, Character_Bible.md, or Environment_Bible.md describes what a "registry card" looks like; Master_Project_Bible mentions "roster-card massacre device (registry stamping students REWRITTEN)" only as a Volume 1 plot beat (Ch.4–8 range), giving a narrative concept but no visual design. | **Design gap, not a durability gap** — needs a UI/graphic design pass (what does a registry card look like: paper? screen? Etude-adjacent parchment? System-cyan HUD per FX-HUD's "thin cyan-white vectors"?) before it can be generated at all. Lower-stakes than the Moderator or monster gaps since it's an inanimate UI element, not a character, but still genuinely unspec'd. This falls slightly outside this cycle's kit-spec scope (which was character-kits + Moderator) and is flagged here as a new, smaller gap rather than resolved. | Cannot build a full packet — no visual language exists yet for "registry card." Recommend a light PROPOSAL: since this is adjacent to "System cyan #9be8ff on black" (Art_Direction's accent-system line for "System") and to FX-HUD's cyan-white vector language, a registry card UI could reasonably borrow that established cyan/black system-aesthetic rather than inventing a fresh visual language — but this is explicitly a proposal for creator taste-call, not an asserted design. | Cannot be scheduled for full creator approval until a UI design proposal (even a minimal one) is drafted and approved — recommend a small follow-up task rather than blocking the whole batch on it, since it's simpler than a character or environment gap. |
| **KF-16** — title logo frame | S16 | No character/environment asset needed — this is a graphic-design/typography still (logo assembled from crack shards, per storyboard: "PATCH 10.0 — glitch settle, cyan seam underline"). The "assembled from crack shards" treatment is a step up from a plain Pillow card and would need the sky-crack visual language (Art_Direction, locked) as its graphic material — reasonably low-effort once that's acknowledged. | Continuity: sky-crack diagonal/branching visual law (locked) applies to the shard-assembly treatment. Typography: no locked font/logo-mark spec currently exists anywhere in the repo for "PATCH 10.0" as a wordmark — this is itself a small open item (is there an approved logotype anywhere? Asset_Registry.md and Video_Workflow.md's card recipe both describe generic Pillow-rendered text, not a designed logo). Flagging this as a minor open item, not a hard block. | Per-KF creator approval. Low-to-moderate risk; the open item is whether a dedicated logotype design is wanted (relates to Q-14) or a well-executed typographic treatment (DejaVuSans-Bold per existing Pillow recipe) suffices. |

---

## Cross-cutting dependency summary (referencing Deliverable 1)

- **KF-04** and (optionally, for characterization value) **KF-06** depend on kits spec'd in `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md` (Cha v3, Si-woo respectively).
- **KF-13** depends on the Moderator design in `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md` (KIT-CHAR-006) — the single heaviest dependency in the whole batch.
- **KF-07** and **KF-08** (attacker half only) depend on a monster design that is NOT covered by `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md` (out of that document's scope — flagged here as a new, previously-unrecorded gap).
- **KF-15** depends on a UI/registry-card design, likewise not covered by `Kit_Specs_DRAFT.md` and newly flagged here.
- **KF-03, KF-04 (environment half), KF-12** depend on environment plates the `Environment_Bible.md` itself already lists as not-yet-generated (8.0 settlement-wide, 9.0 post-patch street variants, murim-gate-through-subway palimpsest set-piece). These are pre-existing, documented gaps — this plan does not discover them, it connects them explicitly to specific KFs for the first time.
- **Q-13** (PMU ch.143 references) is not a hard blocker for any KF above — it was never invoked as a required reference by any shot in the storyboard. No action needed on it from this plan.
- **Q-14** (key-visual poster) is adjacent to KF-08 (explicitly flagged by the storyboard as a "poster shot" candidate) and KF-16 (logo/title), but this plan does not assume Q-14 is authorized — it only notes the overlap for the creator's awareness.

## Ready order

**Tier 1 — ready to generate the moment I-0001/TASK-0003 clears, no kit or environment gap (5 KFs):**
KF-01 (crack, thin) · KF-02 (crack, grown + Han silhouette) · KF-09 (nine rings) · KF-11 (implant wireframe) · KF-14 (Han shoulder roll/smile).
All five use only Han's already-fully-locked kit and/or the sky-crack visual law, which needs no plate at all. These are the batch's genuine "first movers."

**Tier 2 — ready once a durability-cleared LOCKED asset is used correctly but with one open environment-specificity question each (1 KF):**
KF-10 (qi calligraphy slash) — fully unblocked on character/effects grounds; only needs a location decision (grounded vs. abstracted background).

**Tier 3 — hard-blocked on a missing KIT from Deliverable 1 (2 KFs, 1 optional-upgrade):**
KF-04 (Cha v3 kit required for the split-seam pose) · KF-13 (Moderator design required, in addition to its environment blocker below) · KF-06 (optional upgrade — technically shootable with an unnamed extra, but recommended to wait for Si-woo's kit for characterization value).

**Tier 4 — hard-blocked on a missing ENVIRONMENT plate not covered by any kit spec (3 KFs, 1 shared with Tier 3):**
KF-03 (8.0 desert wide + 9.0 converting-street) · KF-12 (9.0 street + 2.0 murim-pass, needed as a first+last PAIR — the storyboard's own designated centerpiece, and currently the least production-ready "money shot" in the batch) · KF-13 (shares its environment blocker with the Moderator-design blocker above — doubly blocked).

**Tier 5 — hard-blocked on a design gap outside this cycle's scope entirely (2 KFs, plus KF-08's attacker half):**
KF-07 (monster silhouette — no monster design exists anywhere in the repo, at any status) · KF-15 (registry-card UI — no visual language exists for it) · KF-08's attacker half only (Han's own half is Tier 1-adjacent per the PROPOSAL noted in its row, if the creator accepts shooting him in isolation).

**Tier 6 — lowest urgency / most flexible (1 KF):**
KF-05 (festival crowd freeze) — blocked only on a lighting/variant decision on an EXISTING plate (ENV-003's "festival-lit" variant, per Environment_Bible's own anticipated-variant checklist) and a minor character-legibility clarification; comparatively cheap to unblock once I-0001 clears.

**Not yet gated (informational, not a KF row):**
KF-16 (title logo) sits closest to already-existing production capability (Pillow card recipe) and is not meaningfully "blocked" the way Tiers 3–5 are — it has one open creative question (dedicated logotype vs. typographic treatment) but no missing kit/plate/design dependency.

## Recommended new TASK_QUEUE / OPEN_QUESTIONS items (for ① EP or the creator to formalize — not created by this plan)
This plan does not add rows to TASK_QUEUE.md or OPEN_QUESTIONS.md beyond what this work order's turn-mechanics section explicitly authorizes (TASK-0006/TASK-0007 cell updates only). The following gaps surfaced above are NOT yet tracked anywhere and are flagged here for ① EP or the creator to formalize as new TASK/OPEN_QUESTIONS rows in a future turn, since adding them now would exceed this turn's write-lane instruction:
1. Monster/extinction-"content" visual design (blocks KF-07, KF-08's attacker half) — currently has zero spec, not even a Character_Bible TODO line.
2. Registry-card UI design (blocks KF-15) — currently has zero visual spec.
3. Missing 8.0 settlement-wide + 9.0 post-patch damaged-street + murim-gate-through-subway palimpsest environment plates (blocks KF-03, KF-04's environment half, KF-12) — these are already-documented gaps in `Environment_Bible.md`'s own "Locations still needing plates" list; this plan is the first place they are tied to specific, named trailer keyframes and prioritized by dramatic importance (KF-12 is the storyboard's designated centerpiece and currently its least-ready shot).
4. Whether FX-PATCH's terrain-conversion grammar may extend to a character subject (KF-04) and whether a new FX-ETUDE-FAIL state should be formally added to Effects_Bible (KF-06) — both are effects-grammar extension questions, not just asset gaps.
